content.problemStatement:
CodeChef has been very busy with his Christmas preparations and he doesn't have time to look after Samosa Bhai and Jalebi Bai. To keep them busy, CodeChef has given them an array **A** of size **N**. He has asked them to plant trees at the points with Cartesian coordinates (**A[ i ]**, **A[ j ]**), such that **i** < **j**.

There are a lot of giraffes nearby. To save the trees from the giraffes, they decide to build a fence around the trees. Moreover, they want to use the minimum length of fencing for this task. Find the value equal to **twice the area** covered by the fence using the minimum length of fencing.


Input Format
The first line contains **T**, the number of test cases to follow.


The first line of each test case contains an integer **N**, the size of the array.


The second line of the test case contains **N** space-separated integers.



Output Format
For each test case, output the value equal to **two times the area**, rounded to the nearest integer, surrounded by the fence when using the minimum length of net to surround all the trees.
constraints: 1 ≤ Sum of N over all cases ≤ 2*105
content.inputFormat: The first line contains T, the number of test cases to follow. The first line of each test case contains an integer N, the size of the array. The second line of the test case contains N space-separated integers.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output the value equal to twice the area covered by the fence when using the minimum length of net to surround all the trees, rounded to the nearest integer.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Example case 1. Covered portion is a right-angled triangle with vertices (2,4), (2,1), and (4,1). Area = (1/2)*2*3 = 3. Example case 2. A, B, C, D, E, and F denote the trees. The above image denotes the situation. Area of the figure = 6.5. 2*(Area of Triangle ABC + Area of Triangle BFE + Area of Square EFCD) is the answer. Area of Triangle ABC = 1.5. Area of Triangle BFE = 1. Area of Square EFCD = 2*2 = 4.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we will use the Graham Scan algorithm to find the convex hull of the given points. The convex hull is the smallest convex polygon that encloses all the points. We will then calculate the area of the convex hull using the Shoelace formula. Finally, we will multiply the area by 2 and round it to the nearest integer to get the final answer.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T*N*log(N)) — The time complexity is dominated by the Graham Scan algorithm, which has a time complexity of O(N*log(N)) for each test case. Since there are T test cases, the overall time complexity is O(T*N*log(N)).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) — The space complexity is O(N) because we need to store the points and the convex hull.

code.Python3:
import math

class Point:
    def __init__(self, x, y):
        self.x = x
        self.y = y

def orientation(p, q, r):
    val = (q.y - p.y) * (r.x - q.x) - (q.x - p.x) * (r.y - q.y)
    if val == 0: return 0  # Collinear
    return 1 if val > 0 else 2  # Clockwise or counterclockwise

def convex_hull(points):
    n = len(points)
    l = 0
    for i in range(1, n):
        if points[i].x < points[l].x: l = i
    p = l
    hull = []
    while True:
        hull.append(points[l])
        q = (p + 1) % n
        for i in range(n):
            if orientation(points[l], points[i], points[q]) == 2: q = i
        l = q
        p = (p + 1) % n
        if p == 0: break
    return hull

def polygon_area(points):
    area = 0.0
    for i in range(len(points)):
        j = (i + 1) % len(points)
        area += points[i].x * points[j].y
        area -= points[j].x * points[i].y
    area = abs(area) / 2.0
    return area

t = int(input())
while t > 0:
    n = int(input())
    points = [Point(*map(int, input().split())) for _ in range(n)]
    hull = convex_hull(points)
    area = polygon_area(hull)
    print(round(2 * area))
    t -= 1
